---
title: Structure
---

A *Calepin* website is a directory containing a `_calepin.toml` manifest and `.qmd` source files. Here is the structure of the *Calepin* documentation website itself:

```{sh}
tree website -I '_site|*_files|*_cache|*.html|*.qmd|*.tex|*.typ' --dirsfirst -L 4
```

# Key components

| Path | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `_calepin.toml` | Site manifest: metadata, navbar, sidebar, pages, brand, format options |
| `_calepin/` | Project-level overrides for templates, elements, and assets |
| `_site/` | Default output directory (generated, should be in `.gitignore`) |
| `_calepin_files/` | Generated figures and supporting files (per-document) |
| `_calepin_cache/` | Execution cache (per-document) |

# Manifest

The `_calepin.toml` file configures the site. See [Sites](../templates/sites.qmd) for the full reference. The main sections are:

| Section | Controls |
|---------|----------|
| `project` | Type, resources to copy |
| `website` | Title, subtitle, URL, favicon, navbar, sidebar, pages |
| `brand` | Colors, logos, typography |
| `format` | Output format options (toc, highlighting, code display) |
| `execute` | Code execution defaults (cache) |

# Pages

The `website.pages` list defines the sidebar navigation. Pages can be flat entries, labeled entries, or nested sections:

```yaml
website:
  pages:
    - index.qmd
    - text: "Custom Label"
      href: about.qmd
    - section: "Guide"
      pages:
        - guide/intro.qmd
        - guide/advanced.qmd
```

Page titles are extracted from YAML front matter. Use `text:` to override the navigation label. Files prefixed with `_` are excluded from rendering.
